claims.evidence_text: The reported values of K vary between 0.28 and 0.90 with a median value of 0.59 (Table 1).
claims.claim_summary: Field-measured wheat canopy light extinction coefficient K ranges 0.28-0.90 (median 0.59)

claims.evidence_text: The values of Kcst used in wheat GCMs range from 0.44 to 0.80 (Table 2), with a median value of 0.52, that is, close to that calculated from field measurements.
claims.claim_summary: Constant light extinction coefficient used in wheat crop models ranges 0.44-0.80 (median 0.52)
